messages.content: Hey everyone! 😊 Random question: what was your absolute favorite game to play as a kid? I was just thinking about some old-school games and feeling nostalgic.

Oh man, I loved playing hide and seek in the neighborhood until it got dark! 😂 We’d always have that one kid who hid way too well and everyone would have to give up. Those were the days! Anyone else have a go-to game?

Haha, hide and seek was the best! But I think my favorite was definitely tag. We’d come up with all these weird variations, like freeze tag or zombie tag. I swear we could run for hours and never get tired 😂 Anyone else remember playing kickball at recess?

Oh my gosh, yes to all of those! I feel like my childhood was basically just endless rounds of tag and hide and seek. 😂 But honestly, I was obsessed with hopscotch. I used to draw the boards with chalk everywhere—my parents probably still find random faded hopscotch lines on our driveway lol. Did anyone else play four square? That was a big deal at my school!

Four square was HUGE at my school too! I remember everyone getting so competitive over the rules, like whether “cherry bombs” were allowed or not 😂 I was never very good but it was still super fun. Did anyone ever play Red Rover? That game got intense sometimes!

Red Rover was wild! 😂 I feel like someone always ended up on the ground or with a bruised arm, but we kept playing anyway. Anyone else remember playing Sardines? It was like reverse hide and seek—once you found the person hiding, you had to cram in with them. I swear we’d all be packed into a tiny closet just giggling until the last person found us.

Sardines! I totally forgot about that one 😂 We used to play it at sleepovers, and it always ended up with everyone squished in some ridiculous hiding spot. I think my top favorite was probably capture the flag—especially when we played it at night during summer camp. Nothing beat that adrenaline rush of sneaking around in the dark trying not to get caught! Anyone else ever get way too into dodgeball in gym class?

Oh my gosh, dodgeball was both the best and the most terrifying 😂 I swear the gym floor was like lava when those foam balls started flying. I was always the one hiding in the back, hoping not to get targeted first. Anyone else remember playing “Mother May I?” or “Simon Says?” Those games felt so intense when you were little—like, the power of “Mother” was real 😂

Oh yes, “Mother May I?” was such a classic! I remember trying to sneak in those extra steps hoping nobody noticed 😅 And “Simon Says” always got me tripped up because I’d get too hyped and forget to listen for the “Simon says” part lol. Did anyone else ever play Duck Duck Goose? That game always ended in chaos with everyone running around in circles!

Duck Duck Goose! That game was pure chaos, especially when someone picked the fastest kid to chase them 😂 You had to be ready to sprint at any second. Anyone else remember heads up, seven up? I always tried to peek at people’s shoes under the desk, but I was terrible at guessing who tapped me. Those rainy day classroom games just hit different!

Heads up, seven up was the best rainy day distraction! 😂 I always got way too into trying to guess who it was, but I feel like I was wrong 90% of the time. Did anyone else play “Telephone”? It was hilarious how the final message always ended up being something totally random, nothing like what it started as. I miss how simple and fun those games were!

Oh, Telephone was hilarious! 😂 I feel like we always started with something super normal and by the end it would be some totally weird sentence that made no sense at all. Honestly, I kind of miss how creative we got with games—remember making up our own rules or inventing new ones when we got bored of the classics? Sometimes the made up games were the best! Did anyone ever make up their own board games or card games with friends?

Omg yes, we definitely made up our own games! My cousins and I once invented a card game called “Monster Mayhem” that only made sense to us, but we played it for years 😂 Also, anyone else remember making obstacle courses in the backyard with random stuff like jump ropes and hula hoops? It was like our own mini Olympics! I kinda miss that creativity.

Backyard obstacle courses were the best! I used to line up chairs, boxes, and whatever random stuff I could find, then challenge my siblings to see who could get through it fastest 😂 I swear, we were all convinced we were training for some big competition. Did anyone else ever try to make a “spy training course” or pretend you were on one of those adventure shows? I totally wanted to be on Legends of the Hidden Temple or something!

Haha, Legends of the Hidden Temple was the dream! I definitely set up “temple runs” with couch cushions and blankets in the living room, pretending the floor was lava and everything. And don’t even get me started on pretending to be a contestant on Double Dare—slime and all 😂 It’s wild how we could turn everyday stuff into the most epic games. Did anyone else ever just invent a game on the spot because you were bored, or was that just me?

Oh yeah, making up games on the fly was basically a requirement for surviving boredom 😂 Sometimes the rules made zero sense, but that was half the fun. My friends and I once made a game called “Shark Carpet” where you could only step on pillows or you’d get “eaten” by sharks (aka, the floor). Honestly, I think we spent more time arguing about the rules than actually playing, but it was always a blast. Anyone else get super competitive during these made-up

Oh totally, we took those made-up games way too seriously sometimes 😂 I remember almost getting into full debates over who touched the “safe zone” first or who was actually “out.” We had this one epic hide and seek game that lasted like an hour because someone got so creative with their hiding spot. Honestly, I miss how everything felt like the most important contest ever back then! Anyone else remember turning literally anything—a stick, a rock, a shoelace—into a game or a treasure

Absolutely! We used to make “treasure hunts” with random stuff we found in the yard all the time. I swear, half the fun was making up wild stories about what the “treasure” actually was 😂 And who else made up secret clubs with their friends? Complete with passwords and all, even though the only real rule was “no siblings allowed.” Childhood imagination was unbeatable!

Haha, love all these stories! I gotta admit though, my favorite was always dodgeball. Something about the chaos of dodging and launching those foam balls just never got old 😂 I definitely took it way too seriously during recess, but it was the best way to burn off energy. Plus, nothing beat that feeling when you managed to catch someone’s throw and get them out! Anyone else a dodgeball fan?

Oh, dodgeball was intense! I was always a little scared of getting hit in the face, but I loved the adrenaline 😂 I secretly lived for the moments when the teams would get down to just two people and everyone would start cheering. Also,
